explainer.title: Recon & AI Pipeline Enumeration
explainer.body: Before attacking an AI system, an adversary maps its surface. This phase demonstrates passive and active reconnaissance against QL-Assist: enumerating API endpoints, extracting the service schema, and identifying exposed configuration including model name, authentication scope, and integration points. The service's /admin and /api/schema endpoints are unauthenticated — a common misconfiguration in internal tooling deployed behind network perimeters. The attacker doesn't need credentials; they need only network access to the internal service mesh.
explainer.why_stack_misses: Your EDR sees no process anomaly — this is HTTP traffic to an internal endpoint, indistinguishable from analyst usage. Your SIEM sees no lateral movement — the attacker has not moved laterally yet. Your UEBA sees no behavioral anomaly — the same endpoints are hit by legitimate tools. Recon against AI APIs looks identical to normal usage.

terminal_lines: [+] Integration map: DocuIntel → FraudSentinel lateral path identified
$ curl -s http://ql-assist:8001/api/schema | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); [print(e['method'],e['path'],e['auth']) for e in d['endpoints']]"
[*] All critical endpoints enumerated. Recon complete.

blue.gap_analysis: Standard HTTP GET requests to service endpoints. No authentication required for /admin or /api/schema. Traffic is indistinguishable from automated health checks or developer tooling. Zero SIEM rules cover unauthenticated access to internal AI service configuration endpoints.

control_failures.description: AI service admin endpoint accessible without authentication — exposes model configuration, integration map, and API keys
allow_external_instructions: true in production configuration — intentional vulnerability enabling Phase 3 injection
No alerting rule for unauthenticated access to internal AI service configuration endpoints
